qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in ImageTextPromptValue permits arbitrary file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identifies the same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: the ImageTextPromptValue class in multi_modal_prompt.py:197-199 calls open() on user-provided file paths without validation or sanitization, enabling arbitrary file read. This directly matches the advisory's description of improper validation of URLs/file paths in the retrieved_contexts parameter when handling multimodal inputs.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in ImageTextPromptValue permits unauthorized access to internal network resources
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: While this finding identifies a vulnerability in the same ImageTextPromptValue class, the advisory specifically describes an Arbitrary File Read vulnerability (CWE-22) in file path handling. This finding instead describes SSRF (CWE-918) via URL handling. Although the advisory mentions URLs in the retrieved_contexts parameter, the primary vulnerability type described is path traversal/file read, not SSRF. This represents a different vulnerability class and attack vector.

selected_finding.title: Path traversal in ImageTextPromptValue permits arbitrary file read

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The path-traversal-via-image-path finding identifies the exact vulnerable code and root cause described in the advisory: the ImageTextPromptValue class in multi_modal_prompt.py:197-199 reads files via open() without validation of user-provided paths. The PoC successfully demonstrated arbitrary file read by accessing /tmp/evohunt_test_file.txt and returning its base64-encoded content, satisfying the exploit success predicate for the advisory's claimed impact of arbitrary file read.
evidence: Finding identifies the same vulnerable code location: src/ragas/prompt/multi_modal_prompt.py:197-199 calling open(file_path, 'rb') without validation
Finding matches the advisory's root cause: improper validation of user-supplied paths in the retrieved_contexts parameter when handling multimodal inputs
PoC execution successful: bash run.sh completed with exit code 0
PoC demonstrated exploit success: arbitrary file (/tmp/evohunt_test_file.txt) was read and its content was successfully retrieved as base64
Runtime output confirms: 'PATH TRAVERSAL SUCCESS: File was accessed!' and 'VERIFIED: Arbitrary file was read successfully!'
No special privileges or user interaction required to exploit
High confidentiality impact confirmed: any file accessible to the application process can be read

poc_executions.observed_effect: The PoC successfully created a test file at /tmp/evohunt_test_file.txt with known marker content, executed the vulnerable open() code path on that arbitrary file path, retrieved the file's base64-encoded content, and verified the marker string matched. This demonstrates end-to-end arbitrary file read through the advisory-described vulnerable code path without any path validation or sanitization.
